title: JavaScript
desc: HTML says what a thing is. CSS says how it looks. Neither one calculates, remembers, or decides. That is where the third language comes in — and with it, every mistake that works nine times and breaks on the tenth.

nothing.title: The computer knows nothing
nothing.desc: The first lesson gives you no syntax. It gives you a page that shows the characters instead of the answer, and asks why.

numbers.title: Numbers and text
numbers.desc: Data arrives as text and behaves like numbers everywhere except one place. This section is about that place, and about everything you do with the two.

decisions.title: Decisions
decisions.desc: So far the code did the same thing every time. Here it starts asking — and with that come the mistakes that work nine times and break on the tenth.

repeating.title: Repeating
repeating.desc: Write it a hundred times. You will not manage — and that is exactly the point. This is also where you first freeze your own page.
